entry.title: SAM2410 : Code 500 at init/sync as soon as a CIM extension is added to the standard app
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I'm upgrading from SAM2305 to SAM2410.

Metadata : SAM2410 + OSS note 3543925  - App version 2410.0.2 - MDK : 24-7-6-001

The standard application works perfectly well now and I began to add a few specifics using CIM and extension .

Very simple customization : Only change the Display Name of the app in Global/Passport and change some translation i18n files. Nothing dangerous.

Since I deployed the "CIM application" , I have issues with a 500 error code, in the client, at initialization or at Synchronization.

Nothing shows in a /IWFND/TRACES. No warning/error in the BTP logs.

I'm working with Business Application Studio and the deployment of the "CIM app" is OK (Bundle OK, update of the app in Mobile Services).

App is tested on iOS devices.

Any help ? any ideas ?

I have a clientlog but it's too big a file to post it

entry.title: Re: Upload Document Reference Information via DTW
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible. you should use RDR21 template.

I am attaching the template.

DTW Template Fields Explained:

🔹 ParentKey (DocNum): The document number where you want to add the referenced document. (Example: 91490549)

🔹 DocEntry: The DocEntry of the ParentKey document (Example: DocEntry of 91490549)

🔹 RefDocEnt: The DocEntry of the document you want to reference.

🔹 RefObjType: The Object Type (ObjType) of the document you want to reference.

With this setup, DTW will correctly link the referenced document to your target document.

entry.title: Re: Upload Document Reference Information via DTW RDR21
entry.content:
Hello

The error is because you don't have all the required data in the template.

DTW Template Fields Explained:

🔹 ParentKey (DocNum): The document number where you want to add the referenced document. (Example: 91490549)

🔹 DocEntry: The DocEntry of the ParentKey document (Example: DocEntry of 91490549)

🔹 RefDocEnt: The DocEntry of the document you want to reference.

🔹 RefObjType: The Object Type (ObjType) of the document you want to reference.

With this setup, DTW will correctly link the referenced document to your target document.

entry.title: Re: Exception in Custom logic CX_BLE_RUNTIME_ERROR with a...
entry.content:
" Option 1)
MESSAGE e002(fins_rev_rec) WITH 'IFRS' 'RA1' INTO FINAL(lv_msg) .
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_ble_runtime_error USING MESSAGE.

" Option 2)
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_ble_runtime_error MESSAGE e002(fins_rev_rec) WITH 'IFRS' 'RA1'.

" Option 3) ... error without message class - the pure error text
FINAL(lo_exception) = NEW cx_ble_runtime_error( error_message = 'This is error' ).
RAISE EXCEPTION lo_exception.

 

" Option 4) if you need more parameters then add: attr3 = 'param3' attr4 = 'param4' and then poplulate them with values. The Maximum is 4 parameters.

FINAL(lo_exception2) = NEW cx_ble_runtime_error( textid = value #( msgid = 'FINS_REV_REC' msgno = '002' attr1 = 'param1' attr2 = 'param2' ) param1 = 'IFRS' param2 = 'RA1' ).
RAISE EXCEPTION lo_exception2.

entry.title: SAP CI APIM: Extracting Variables from XML and JSON Data
entry.content:
In this blog, we’ll explore how to extract variables from XML and JSON data using the Extract Variable policy in SAP API Management (APIM). This policy allows you to parse data from requests or responses and assign values to variables for use during API proxy execution.

Extracting Variables from XML Data:

Step 1: Create a Proxy

  1. Open the Proxy and navigate to the Policy section.

 

2. Click Edit in the top-right corner.

3. On the left panel, select either the Proxy Endpoint or Target Endpoint where you want to add this policy.

Step 2: Configure the Extract Variable Policy

In this scenario, we select Proxy Endpoint and attach the Extract Variable policy by clicking “+” on the right side.

Give the policy name and select the stream as Incoming or Outgoing depending on whether it should be applied to the request or response. Here, we use Incoming Request.

A sample code snippet will be generated upon adding the policy.

 

Extract Variable Policy for XML:

 

 

<ExtractVariables async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<XMLPayload>
		<Variable name="idoc_no" type="string">
			<XPath>/orders/order/header/idoc_no</XPath>
		</Variable>
		<Variable name="order_no" type="string">
			<XPath>//order_no</XPath>
		</Variable>
		<Variable name="item" type="nodeset">
			<XPath>//item</XPath>
		</Variable>
	</XMLPayload>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</ExtractVariables>

 

 

 

With the above policy I am extracting the below details.

  • idoc_no: Extracted using the string type to read the exact value inside idoc_no.
  • order_no: Extracted using the string type to capture the exact value inside order_no.
  • item: Extracted using the type as “nodeset” to capture the entire item node along with all its child elements. including line items and their attributes, are read and passed as a complete structure.

Refer to the SAP Documentation: SAP APIM Documentation. to modify the policy as per your requirement.

Step 3: Assign Message Policy

After extracting variables, use the Assign Message policy to store and retrieve values. you can use any other policy as per your requirement.

 

Sample Assign Message Policy for XML:

 

 

<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Set>
		<Payload contentType="application/xml" variablePrefix="@" variableSuffix="#">
			<Orders>
				<idoc_no>@idoc_no#</idoc_no>
				<order_no>@order_no#</order_no>
				@item#
			</Orders>
		</Payload>
	</Set>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<AssignTo createNew="false" type="request"> request</AssignTo>
</AssignMessage>

 

 

 

We must specify the type as either "request" or "response" depending on where the policy is applied. Since the policy is being used on the Request here, the type is set as "request".

 

 

<AssignTo createNew="false" type="request"> request</AssignTo>

 

 

 

Step 4: Testing the XML Extraction

  1. Deploy the API Proxy.

  2. Copy the API Proxy URL and test it using Postman.

  1. Pass the Content-Type as application/xml (mandatory) in the Headers.

  2. Use the sample XML payload to validate the extraction.

Below is the sample Input Payload:

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<orders>
	<order>
		<header>
			<idoc_no>888888</idoc_no>
			<order_no>5000123456</order_no>
			<sold_to>AAAA-111</sold_to>
			<ship_to>BBBB-222</ship_to>
			<order_date>20211201</order_date>
		</header>
		<item>
			<line_item>00001</line_item>
			<material>800111</material>
			<quantity_uom>pce</quantity_uom>
			<weight>20.2</weight>
			<weight_uom>kgm</weight_uom>
			<schedule>
				<line>
					<schedule_qty>10</schedule_qty>
					<schedule_date>20211210</schedule_date>
				</line>
			</schedule>
		</item>
		<item>
			<line_item>00002</line_item>
			<material>800222</material>
			<quantity_uom>pce</quantity_uom>
			<weight>200.3</weight>
			<weight_uom>kgm</weight_uom>
			<schedule>
				<line>
					<schedule_qty>40</schedule_qty>
					<schedule_date>20211210</schedule_date>
				</line>
				<line>
					<schedule_qty>60</schedule_qty>
					<schedule_date>20211220</schedule_date>
				</line>
			</schedule>
		</item>
	</order>
</orders>

 

 

Output Payload Received:

<Orders>
    <idoc_no>888888</idoc_no>
    <order_no>5000123456</order_no>
    <item>
        <line_item>00001</line_item>
        <material>800111</material>
        <quantity_uom>pce</quantity_uom>
        <weight>20.2</weight>
        <weight_uom>kgm</weight_uom>
        <schedule>
            <line>
                <schedule_qty>10</schedule_qty>
                <schedule_date>20211210</schedule_date>
            </line>
        </schedule>
    </item>
    <item>
        <line_item>00002</line_item>
        <material>800222</material>
        <quantity_uom>pce</quantity_uom>
        <weight>200.3</weight>
        <weight_uom>kgm</weight_uom>
        <schedule>
            <line>
                <schedule_qty>40</schedule_qty>
                <schedule_date>20211210</schedule_date>
            </line>
            <line>
                <schedule_qty>60</schedule_qty>
                <schedule_date>20211220</schedule_date>
            </line>
        </schedule>
    </item>
</Orders>

 

Extracting Variables from JSON Data

Step 1: Configure the Extract Variable Policy for JSON

In the same way we can extract variables from the JSON data as well to extract the JSON use the below format in the policy.

 

 

 

<ExtractVariables async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
    <JSONPayload>
		<Variable name="idoc_no">
		    <JSONPath>$.orders[0].header.idoc_no</JSONPath>
		</Variable>
		<Variable name="order_no">
		    <JSONPath>$.orders[0].header.order_no</JSONPath>
		</Variable>
		<Variable name="item">
		    <JSONPath>$.orders[*].item</JSONPath>
		</Variable>
	</JSONPayload>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</ExtractVariables>

 

 

 

Step 2: Assign Message Policy for JSON

 

 

 

 

<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Set>
		<Payload contentType="application/json" variablePrefix="@" variableSuffix="#">
		    {
				"orders": [
					{
						"header": {
							"idoc_no": "@idoc_no#",
							"order_no": "@order_no#",
							}
						"item": @item#
			
					}
				]
			}
		</Payload>
	</Set>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<AssignTo createNew="false" type="request">request</AssignTo>
</AssignMessage>

 

 

Step 3: Testing the JSON Extraction

  1. Deploy the API Proxy.

  2. Copy the API Proxy URL and test it using Postman.

  3. Pass the Content-Type as application/json (mandatory).

 

JSON Input payload Used:

 

 

 

{
    "orders": [
        {
            "header": {
                "idoc_no": "888888",
                "order_no": "5000123456",
                "sold_to": "AAAA-111",
                "ship_to": "BBBB-222",
                "order_date": "20211201"
            },
            "item": [
                {
                    "line_item": "00001",
                    "material": "800111",
                    "quantity_uom": "pce",
                    "weight": 20.2,
                    "weight_uom": "kgm",
                    "schedule": [
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 10,
                            "schedule_date": "20211210"
                        }
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "line_item": "00002",
                    "material": "800222",
                    "quantity_uom": "pce",
                    "weight": 200.3,
                    "weight_uom": "kgm",
                    "schedule": [
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 40,
                            "schedule_date": "20211210"
                        },
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 60,
                            "schedule_date": "20211220"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

 

 

 

JSON Output:

 

 

 

{
    "orders": [
        {
            "header": {
                "idoc_no": "888888",
                "order_no": "5000123456",
            }
						"item": [
                {
                    "line_item": "00001",
                    "material": "800111",
                    "quantity_uom": "pce",
                    "weight": 20.2,
                    "weight_uom": "kgm",
                    "schedule": [
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 10,
                            "schedule_date": "20211210"
                        }
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "line_item": "00002",
                    "material": "800222",
                    "quantity_uom": "pce",
                    "weight": 200.3,
                    "weight_uom": "kgm",
                    "schedule": [
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 40,
                            "schedule_date": "20211210"
                        },
                        {
                            "schedule_qty": 60,
                            "schedule_date": "20211220"
                        }
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

 

 

 

Conclusion

By following this approach, you can extract elements from both XML and JSON data using the Extract Variable Policy and assign them using the Assign Message Policy in SAP API Management.

For further reference:

  • SAP APIM Documentation: SAP APIM

  • Google Apigee: Apigee Docs

I hope this guide was helpful. Feel free to leave any feedback or questions in the comments. Happy learning!

entry.title: Enhancing Efficiency with Snowflake Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Challenges with SAP’s Native Snowflake Adapter for SAP Integration Suite

While SAP Integration Suite provides a framework for integration, its native Snowflake adapter presents several challenges that can complicate data workflows. Users may encounter limited authentication options, requiring manual credential management, which poses security risks. Query management is cumbersome, with no intuitive interface for browsing database structures or generating SQL queries automatically. Additionally, data ingestion is restricted by batch size limitations, affecting performance when handling large datasets. Query execution is limited to receiver-side operations, leading to inefficiencies when processing high-volume data. Lastly, the lack of real-time Change Data Capture (CDC) makes it difficult to maintain synchronized data across SAP and Snowflake environments.

Seamless Authentication with OAuth 2.0

Advantco’s Snowflake adapter simplifies authentication with full support for OAuth 2.0, ensuring secure and automated access to Snowflake. With an intuitive Workbench interface, users can configure and manage authentication effortlessly, eliminating the need for manual credential handling and enhancing security.

Figure 1: OAuth 2.0 configuration in Workbench

Figure 2: Reuse connection configuration and monitor referencing integration flows

 

Intuitive Database Explorer for Effortless Query Management

The integration process becomes significantly smoother with Advantco’s Database Explorer, which provides a structured and user-friendly way to interact with Snowflake databases. Users can browse database structures, generate SQL queries, and automate schema creation, reducing manual effort and minimizing errors.

Figure 3: Database Explorer and Schema/Payload generator

 

Figure 4: SQL Query Builder

 

High-Performance Data Ingestion with Snowpipe Streaming API

Handling large-scale data ingestion efficiently is crucial for enterprises. Advantco’s Snowflake adapter leverages Snowpipe Streaming API, allowing businesses to stream data continuously without batch size limitations. This approach reduces latency, enhances performance, and optimizes cost by eliminating the need for constant compute warehouse usage.

Figure 5: Example of batch streaming from Advantco Kafka adapter to Snowflake adapter

 

Optimized Query Processing with Sender-Side Execution

Efficient data retrieval is essential for smooth operations. Advantco’s Snowflake adapter enables sender-side query execution, allowing for scheduled polling, pagination, and optimized record processing. This ensures that even large datasets execute smoothly without causing integration delays.

Figure 6: Query configuration

Figure 7: Scheduler and Pagination

 

Real-Time Change Data Capture (CDC) for Data Synchronization

Keeping data synchronized between SAP and Snowflake is simplified with real-time Change Data Capture (CDC). Advantco’s Snowflake adapter tracks inserts, updates, and deletions efficiently, ensuring that businesses maintain up-to-date data across their systems without performance degradation.

Figure 8: CDC configuration

 

Conclusion: The enhanced Advantco Snowflake adapter for SAP Integration Suite provides:

  • Secure OAuth 2.0 Authentication – Reduces the need for credential management.
  • Database Explorer – Automates query generation and schema management.
  • High-Performance Streaming – Supports real-time data ingestion with Snowpipe.
  • Optimized Query Execution – Handles large datasets efficiently.
  • Real-Time CDC Support – Synchronizes SAP and Snowflake environments.

entry.title: Update invoice status using API
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

I have to create invoices using API calls to SAP. 

There are 2 type of invoices:

  • Invoices with PO reference
  • Invoices with a GL / CC reference

For the invoices with a GL / CC reference, how can I POST them in SAP with a specific status (BLOCKED), then an approbation is made in an external system to approve that specific invoice. Is that a specific endpoint, or do I have to make a new POST call with a new 'SupplierInvoiceStatus' value?

For the invoice with PO references, we are currently using MRBR. 

I will POST the invoice with an API call, it will be block by MRBR (unit price difference...), how can I release that invoice if the approbation is made in an other system? Is there a specific endpoint to approve a blocked invoiced from MRBR, or do I have to update the invoice status and make a new POST with a different value of 'SupplierInvoiceStatus'?

Thanks for your help

entry.title: refresh cco sales screen from a button
entry.content:
Hey customer checkout plugin development experts, 
 
I'm trying to refresh the UI (sales screen) after updating the receipt with some discounts. 
 
I see the changes on the receipt, but can't seem to figure out how to refresh the screen.
 
Here's my popup message with the redeem button code:
 
// Handle when user clicks "Redeem Tickets"
if (StringUtils.equals("redeemTicket", requestId)) {
String materialId = body.getString("materialId");
 
if (currentReceipt != null) {
System.out.println("🎟️ Redeem Ticket Button Clicked");
 
if (currentReceipt == null) {
System.out.println("⚠️ No active receipt object! Fetching latest...");
}
 
if (currentReceipt == null) {
System.out.println("❌ No active receipt found!");
return;
}
ApplyTicket(currentReceipt);
}
 
Here's my ApplyTicket code:
 
public void ApplyTicket(ReceiptEntity crrentReceipt) {
System.out.print("Entered Button Apply Ticket");
 
String receiptKey = currentReceipt.getKey();
 
ReceiptEntity receipt = receiptManager.retrieveEntityByKey(receiptKey);
 
List<SalesItemEntity> salesItems = receipt.getSalesItems();
for (SalesItemEntity salesItem: salesItems) {
 
salesItem.setDiscountNetAmount(BigDecimal.valueOf(4.00));
 
salesItem.setDiscountManuallyChanged(true);
salesItem.setMarkChanged(true);
salesItem.setItemDiscountChanged(true);
}
// receiptManager.update(receipt);
calculationPosService.calculate(receipt, EntityActions.CHECK_CONS);
UIEventDispatcher.INSTANCE.dispatchAction(CConst.UIEventsIds.RECEIPT_REFRESH, null, receipt);
 
for (SalesItemEntity salesItem: salesItems) {
 
System.out.println("Receipt Changes ItemCode: " + salesItem.getId() + " Discount Amount: " + salesItem.getDiscountNetAmount());
}
}
 
and this is my onSalesItemAddedtoReceipt and onSalesItemUpdated methods where I'm setting the global currentReceipt = receipt
 
public  void onSalesItemAddedToReceipt(com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.CDBSession dbSession, com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.ReceiptEntity receipt, java.util.List<com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.SalesItemEntity> salesItems, java.math.BigDecimal quantity) {
 
currentReceipt = receipt;
}
 
 
//this event here when you updating item price, qty etc...
 public void onSalesItemUpdated(com.sap.scco.ap.pos.dao.CDBSession dbSession, com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.ReceiptEntity receipt, com.sap.scco.ap.pos.entity.SalesItemEntity newSalesItem, java.math.BigDecimal quantity) {
 
System.out.println("Entered Default onsalesItemUpdated");
currentReceipt = receipt;
}
 
As you can see, at the end of ApplyTicket method, I'm logging the receipt changes and they are correct, but I can't see to figure out how to update the Sales UI.
 
I would really appreciate your help to resolve this as soon as possible.
 
Thanks

entry.title: Schedule Margin Key (SMK) Not Functioning/Driving Early MRP Requirements
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking for two-pronged support:

1. I am currently trying to get schedule margin keys implemented in an effort to drive material requirements ahead of planned need (based on existing IHPT), without using either GR processing time or increasing IHPTs. I have schedule margin keys configured to add 20 days (separately) to opening period, float after production, float before production, and release period. I have configured materials with the different SMKs (except float after production), but none are driving changes to the component requirement dates. Does anyone have an idea as to what may be preventing the SMKs from driving different material requirement dates?

2. If my understanding of SMKs is incorrect and they cannot be configured to drive early material requirements without changing IHPTs, what other methods exist to accomplish this end goal? I understand you can apply individual component lead time offsets, but the bills of materials I have are long and I have a lot of final assembly materials so it's an extensive effort. I have also looked at applying safety time but, again, it didn't seem to work similarly to the SMKs.

Thank you.

entry.title: Absolute Price Limit is not populating on freight order under subcontracting / Tender Execuiton.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

During the implementation of broadcast tendering, we encountered an issue where the absolute price limit is not populating on freight orders that are publishing broadcast tenders. When you look at the subcontracting tab for any tenders that are for broadcast tender, you will see the absolute price limit column is blank where it should be displaying the carrier"s price limit.

Additionally, the carrier portal does not allow carriers to adjust the price limit for the specific load. In the visibility settings, the Price Lim. is set to RO (Read Only) and Subm. Price is set to ER (Editable, Relevant). Please see the attached document for reference. Acceptance of the tender results in rejection due to the price exceeding the limit.

This issue is not present in our Dev and Test systems.

entry.title: SAP Note 1538088 - Tax Calculation in Brazil
entry.content:
Olá pessoal!

Hoje, quero falar sobre uma nota SAP que considero importante para nós:
1538088 - Tax Calculation in Brazil com a sua última versão em 30.01.2024.

Essa nota fala sobre os tipos de esquemas de cálculos dentro do SAP para o Brasil e é claro que encontraremos a TAXBRJ e RVXBRA (esquema clássico) e a TAXBRA e RVABRA (esquema baseado em condições - CBT).

Nessa nota, o que me chama atenção é a instrução de uso para as empresas de mining, utilities e oil que seriam:

* SAP Mining: TAXBRA/ RVABRA
* IS Oil: TAXBRA/ RVABRA, se você vender SOMENTE petroleum (upstream);
TAXBRJ/ RVXBRA se for upstream e vender derivados do petroleum e o petroleo. Mistream e Downstream.
* IS-Utilities: TAXBRA/ RVABRA

SAP Women in Tech SAP S/4HANA Logistics for Brazil SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) for SAP S/4HANA

GC is my group currency value, [d/Time] is my Time dimension type

(Depending on the scenario) Otherwise look at fields like VBELN billing document, VBEL2 sales document, POSNR sales document item, or reference fields like XBLNR or ZUONR.

[Rich] Hey friends, Rich Heilman here. Last week, I told you about the release of Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities, and I just wanted it to make you aware of some two-minute of ABAP cloud videos that we are publishing over the next few weeks. These videos are specifically around the Joule for developer ABAP AI capabilities and should give you a quick overview of what it is and how to use it and some tips and tricks as well. I've created a special playlist on the developer's YouTube channel for your convenience, and I'll post a link in the comments as well. The first few are out there now with a fun little introduction, and then we get right into the good stuff in the next episodes of the series, introducing you to Joule and how to get started using it today. These demos are coming straight out of our development organization by our developers for our developers. So check them out today. Bye for now.

You can join the global webinar on SAP Integration Suite onboarding on March 18th, 2025 from 3 to 4PM CET. This session is geared towards all personas looking to get started with SAP Integration Suite to help drive your digital transformation. You will get an overview of all the available SAP Integration Suite capabilities and learn how to activate them. The session covers frequently asked questions, fundamentals and beginner tips to help you get started with SAP Integration Suite. We look forward to seeing you at this event. The registration link for this webinar is available in the description.

[Mamikee] This Saturday, March 1st, 2025, SAP Inside Track, Bengaluru is bringing together developers, experts, and innovators for a full day of learning, networking, and hands-on AI experiences live and in-person. This is your chance to explore cutting-edge AI solutions across SAP BTP, S/4 HANA, Ariba, concur, and Success Factor. You'll hear from directly from top SAP experts about the future of Enterprise AI and discover real-run success stories from businesses already leveraging AI to drive innovation. You'll get handled experience in interactive workshops, explore live demos, and see how AI seamlessly integrates into SAP solutions. This is your opportunity to connect with SAP professionals, developers, and AI inclusives all in one place. And remember, this is a in-person-only event, so don't miss out. You can secure your spot in the link below.

[Rich] Wait, wait, wait, wait. I'm still Rich Heilman, and I forgot to tell you about a new blog post from our good friend Andre Fischer. And in his blog post, Andre shows us how to build a responsive user interface for RAP business objects that start as an asynchronous process. So in this blog post, Andre shows us an easy way to start that process using the background process framework and shows us an easy way to update the user interface with the result thanks to a new feature which has recently been released which allows to refresh the user interface of an SAP Fiori Elements application by simply raising an event. So very cool stuff here. Make sure to check out Andre's blog post today and I'll link to it in the description and bye for now. And I'm really going to go this time. Bye-bye. Thank you.

entry.title: Custom routine for pricing procedure not performing calculation
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I am doing some experimentation and trying to create a custom routine. I am not familiar with the process and running into some problems at the moment. In my code below, I am trying to give a user a discount if in the current month they have more than 3 orders over the value of 3000. I see some people use "komp" in their code, but what is this and how can it be implemented in my scenario? Can someone please shed some light as to where I am going wrong?

DATA: lv_order_count        TYPE i,
      lv_total_value        TYPE decimal10_2,
      lv_discount           TYPE decimal10_2,
      lv_discounted_value   TYPE decimal10_2,
      lt_vbak               TYPE TABLE OF vbak,
      ls_vbak               TYPE vbak,
      lv_first_day_of_month TYPE sy-datum.

lv_first_day_of_month = sy-datum(6) && '01'.

SELECT * FROM vbak INTO TABLE lt_vbak
   WHERE erdat BETWEEN lv_first_day_of_month AND sy-datum and netwr > 3000 AND kunnr = '1987653'.

lv_order_count = LINES( lt_vbak ).

 LOOP AT lt_vbak INTO ls_vbak.
    IF lv_order_count > 3.
      lv_discount = ls_vbak-netwr * '0.03'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Re: Not able to download the SAP ASE for Linux 64bit 16.0...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Please make sure you are trying to download from the correct link. See KBA 2469889 - How to download current ASE patches - SAP ASE:

  1. Access Software Downloads
  2. Pick middle tab "SUPPORT PACKAGES & PATCHES"

*NOTE: Do NOT use download media from 'Installation' Section of the download pages. These are outdated versions.          

  1. "By Alphabetical Index (A-Z)"
  2. "A"
  3. "SAP ADAPTIVE SERVER ENTERPRISE"
  4. "SAP ASE 16.0.4"                                                                   
  5. "SAP ASE SERVER 16.0.4"                                                                  
  6. Select the Operating System from dropdown list (ie "LINUX ON X86_64  64BIT")
  7. There is a list of ASE 16.0 SPXX PLXX  with .TGZ file extensions
  8. Select the version needed:

ASESERV16004P06HF_1-80008862.TGZ

 

Let me know if this helps.

 

Kind Regards,

Gabriel

entry.title: Re: Automatic picking quantity without the picking be con...
entry.content:
Hello @miaguo 

No, you can't do that - that's not logical. Picking status C means that picking quantity = delivery quantity. Once picking quantity = delivery quantity, picking status is set to C. Thus what you are trying to achieve is to purposefully force data inconsistency. Don't do that.

What is the business requirement that you are trying to achieve?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

@tskwin if User is still in your company, he/she should be present in your AD (Azure) and have the same group and if user is left the company - do you still need to provide a access to SAC ?? then how is it Azure AD works with SSO.. ?? still need to know some details ...

Best Practice : 

  • User ABC is in Azure AD with group SAC_Admin
  • User ABC will sync from Azure AD to SAP IAS and Status with Active and Group gets assigned from Azure to IAS Group for that user with SAC_Admin
  • User ABC with group SAC_Admin will identify the user needs a access to SAC.. IPS will do sync from IAS to SAC
    User ABC logins with SAC .. it will be SSO with Azure AD which will be authenticated.
  • User ABC left the company , Azure AD will make status to Inactive or end date
  • User ABC will not be able to authenticate and cannot access the application

entry.title: MRM Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Header

This blog is part of our series Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.

Explore the full series to learn how to streamline exchange rate management across your SAP ecosystem:

  • Intro: Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.
  • Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management in BTP
  • Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
  • Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud.

Introduction

Now that we have successfully set up SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) in BTP, the next step is to automate the exchange rate retrieval process using SAP Integration Suite. In this blog, we'll guide you through creating an integration flow (iFlow) to fetch exchange rate data from MRM and push it to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

This tutorial will walk you through building an integration flow (iFlow) in SAP Integration Suite that:

  1. Fetches exchange rate data from Slovak National Bank (NBS)
    • https://nbs.sk/statisticke-udaje/kurzovy-listok/denny-kurzovy-listok-ecb/
  2. Transforms the data into the SAP MRM JSON format
    • https://help.sap.com/docs/mrm-byor/mrm-byor-service/exchange-rates#upload-exchange-rates-in-the-json-format
  3. Uploads the exchange rates to SAP Market Rates Management

Prerequisites

Before setting up the iFlow, ensure you have:

  • SAP Integration Suite is set up in SAP BTP
  • MRM Service Instance and Service Keys created in BTP
  • API Key for accessing Market Rates Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud configured to receive exchange rate updates

Step-by-Step Guide to Creating an iFlow

 

We will follow these steps to build our iFlow.

  1. Trigger iFlow Daily – Use a Timer Start Event to schedule daily execution.
  2. Fetch Exchange Rates from NBS – Use an HTTP Adapter to call the Slovak National Bank API and retrieve XML data.
    • In our tutorial we will fetch data from https://nbs.sk/statisticke-udaje/kurzovy-listok/denny-kurzovy-listok-ecb/
    • It will return XML with actual exchage rates

Response:

 

 

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<gesmes:Envelope xmlns:gesmes="http://www.gesmes.org/xml/2002-08-01" xmlns="http://www.ecb.int/vocabulary/2002-08-01/eurofxref">
  <gesmes:subject>Reference rates</gesmes:subject>
  <gesmes:Sender>
    <gesmes:name>European Central Bank</gesmes:name>
  </gesmes:Sender>
  <Cube>
    <Cube time="2025-02-27">
      <Cube currency="USD" rate="1,0477"/>
      <Cube currency="JPY" rate="156,73"/>
      <Cube currency="BGN" rate="1,9558"/>
      <Cube currency="CZK" rate="24,987"/>
      <Cube currency="DKK" rate="7,4584"/>
      <Cube currency="GBP" rate="0,82673"/>
      <Cube currency="HUF" rate="399,43"/>
      <Cube currency="PLN" rate="4,1308"/>
      <Cube currency="RON" rate="4,9763"/>
      <Cube currency="SEK" rate="11,159"/>
      <Cube currency="CHF" rate="0,9407"/>
      <Cube currency="ISK" rate="145,5"/>
      <Cube currency="NOK" rate="11,7015"/>
      <Cube currency="TRY" rate="38,1682"/>
      <Cube currency="AUD" rate="1,6638"/>
      <Cube currency="BRL" rate="6,0825"/>
      <Cube currency="CAD" rate="1,504"/>
      <Cube currency="CNY" rate="7,6127"/>
      <Cube currency="HKD" rate="8,1446"/>
      <Cube currency="IDR" rate="17 238,38"/>
      <Cube currency="ILS" rate="3,7184"/>
      <Cube currency="INR" rate="91,311"/>
      <Cube currency="KRW" rate="1 511,87"/>
      <Cube currency="MXN" rate="21,3501"/>
      <Cube currency="MYR" rate="4,6565"/>
      <Cube currency="NZD" rate="1,8434"/>
      <Cube currency="PHP" rate="60,632"/>
      <Cube currency="SGD" rate="1,4049"/>
      <Cube currency="THB" rate="35,522"/>
      <Cube currency="ZAR" rate="19,2947"/>
    </Cube>
  </Cube>
</gesmes:Envelope>

 

 

   3. Parse data – Use Groovy script to convert the data into the required format

We have to parse incoming XML into MRM structure described here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/mrm-byor/mrm-byor-service/exchange-rates#upload-exchange-rates-in-the-json-format

Let’s write simple script to convert data.

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
import groovy.json.JsonOutput;
import groovy.util.XmlParser;
import java.time.LocalDate;
import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Body
    def body = message.getBody(String.class);

    // Parse the XML data
    def parser = new XmlParser();
    def xml = parser.parseText(body);
    def date = xml.Cube.Cube[0].@time;

    // Increase the date by one day
    def formatter = DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd");
    def originalDate = LocalDate.parse(date, formatter);
    def newDate = originalDate.plusDays(1).format(formatter); //because business logic in S4

    def rates = xml.Cube.Cube[0].Cube.collect { node ->
        [
            providerCode     : "Y001",
            marketDataSource : "BYOR",
            marketDataCategory: "01",
            key1             : "EUR",
            key2             : node.@currency,
            marketDataProperty: "CMID",
            effectiveDate    : newDate,
            effectiveTime    : "00:00:00",
            marketDataValue  : node.@rate.replace(',', '.').replace(' ', '').toDouble(),
            fromFactor       : "1",
            toFactor         : "1"
        ]
    }

    def jsonOutput = JsonOutput.toJson(rates);
    message.setBody(JsonOutput.prettyPrint(jsonOutput));

    message.setHeader(''Content-Type'', ''application/json'');

    return message;
}

 

 

 

 

Result:

 

 

 

[
  {
    "providerCode": "Y001",
    "marketDataSource": "BYOR",
    "marketDataCategory": "01",
    "key1": "EUR",
    "key2": "USD",
    "marketDataProperty": "CMID",
    "effectiveDate": "2025-02-28",
    "effectiveTime": "00:00:00",
    "marketDataValue": 1.0477,
    "fromFactor": "1",
    "toFactor": "1"
  },
….
]

 

 

   4. POST data to MRM.
Upload to SAP MRM – Use a POST request via HTTP Adapter to send the formatted exchange rates to SAP Market Rates Management.

 

 

  5. Deploy & Automate – Deploy the iFlow, monitor logs for errors, and schedule it for automatic daily updates.

 

Summary

By the end of this blog, you’ll have a deployed and tested iFlow to automatically fetch exchange rates and prepare them for SAP Public Cloud integration.

entry.title: MRM Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Header

This blog is part of our series Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.

Explore the full series to learn how to streamline exchange rate management across your SAP ecosystem:

  • Intro: Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.
  • Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management in BTP
  • Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
  • Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud.

Introduction

Now that we have successfully set up SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) in BTP and automated exchange rate retrieval via SAP Integration Suite, the final step is to configure SAP Public Cloud to consume these rates and ensure real-time updates.

In this blog, we will:

  1. Set up communication systems between SAP Public Cloud and SAP Integration Suite.
  2. Configure exchange rate management settings in SAP Public Cloud.
  3. Schedule automated updates to ensure continuous synchronization.

By the end of this guide, you’ll have a fully integrated system where exchange rates flow seamlessly from external providers → SAP MRM → SAP Public Cloud.

Step-by-Step Guide to Configuring Exchange Rate Updates

Step 1: Set Up Communication System in SAP Public Cloud

To enable data exchange, we first need to configure communication management.

Navigate to Communication Systems

  1. Log in to SAP Public Cloud
  2. Go to Manage Communication Systems
  3. Click New and enter the following details:
    • System ID: SAP_MRM
    • System Name: SAP Market Rates Management
    • Host: URL from BTP
    • Authentication Method: Basic

Create a Communication Arrangement

  1. Navigate to Manage Communication Arrangements
  2. Click New and select the relevant scenario:
    • Scenario ID: SAP_COM_0203 (Currency Exchange Rates)
    • Communication System: SAP_MRM (created from previous step)
    • Universal Resource Indicator:  DownloadURL (BTP Cockpit)
  3. Configure authentication credentials and service URLs from SAP Integration Suite
  4. Save and activate the arrangement

Step 2: Define currency setup

  1. Go to Assign Currency Notations – Datafeed
  2. Set Data Provider to Y001
  3. Create a new datafeed
  4. Click Save

 

ACN

MRM

ACN  – optional

Currency pair

key1 & key2

 

Rate Type

-

“M”

Data provider

providerCode

 

Instrument

“<key1>~<key2>:<marketDataCategory>”

 

Property

marketDataProperty

 

Data Source

marketDataSource

 

External : datafeed

<fromFactor>: <toFactor>

 

Instrument class

marketDataCategory

 

Step 3: Automate Daily Exchange Rate Updates

To avoid manual imports, we configure a scheduled job for automatic updates.

  1. Navigate to Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs
  2. Click Create New Job
  3. Select Job Template: Import Exchange Rates from MRM
  4. Configure daily execution
  5. Save and activate the job

Summary

This blog covers how to configure SAP Public Cloud to automatically consume and update exchange rates from SAP Market Rates Management (MRM). We set up:

  • Communication Systems to connect SAP Public Cloud and SAP Integration Suite
  • Currency Pairs and Import Settings to enable smooth data flow
  • Scheduled Jobs for daily exchange rate updates
  • Monitoring & Troubleshooting to ensure system stability

By completing this setup, you now have a fully automated exchange rate management process in SAP Public Cloud.

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services - SAML 2.0 Automatic Certificate Renewal [metadata URL is must]
entry.content:
Here's how it works:

  1. First Sign-In Attempt: When a user attempts to sign in and the Identity Authentication detects that the encryption certificate or SAML 2.0 certificate has expired, the sign-in attempt fails.
  2. Automatic Update: The identity authentication system immediately retrieves the metadata URL provided and uses it to update the expired certificates.
  3. Successful Sign-In: Once the certificates are updated, the user can successfully sign in, ensuring uninterrupted access to digital resources.

Metadata URL is must for automatic Renewal of signing and encryption certificates of the application

This automatic update feature offers several benefits:

  • Enhanced Security: By ensuring that certificates are always current, the system prevents potential security breaches that could arise from expired certificates.
  • Improved User Experience: Users experience minimal disruption, as the system handles certificate updates seamlessly in the background.
  • Reduced Administrative Burden: IT administrators no longer need to manually track and update certificates, freeing them to focus on other critical tasks.

In conclusion, the ability to automatically update expired encryption certificates and SAML 2.0 certificates is a game-changer in the realm of identity authentication. It enhances security, improves user experience, and reduces the administrative burden, making it an essential feature for any modern digital infrastructure.

entry.title: Api selection for CLIM catalog
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I've recently embarked on an ISH migration project and need to determine the appropriate service for retrieving aggregated data from the CLIM, CLIF, CLIA, and CLIR catalogs. My goal is to fetch the data, aggregate it in the model layer, and then supply it to a custom view for visualization.

While reviewing the sample programs—specifically RN1API_CLIM_SAMPLE_CATALOG—I observed the use of the following approach:

Solution 1:

  • Package: NMED_API_CLIM
  • Interfaces: IF_ISHMED_API_CLIM1 and IF_ISHMED_API_CLIM2 (the latter includes additional handling for dates and measurement counts)

In contrast, the standard documentation for vital parameters extensions suggests using an alternative service:

Solution 2:

  • Package: NMED_DWS_COMP
  • Interface: IF_ISHMED_IS_API
  • Class: CL_ISHMED_CLIM_API_VS

At first glance, the first solution appears to offer broader capabilities, though it demands more advanced object-oriented programming skills. The second solution, intended for external integration purposes, utilizes a flatter structure.

I’m curious: Are these two approaches equivalent, or do they differ in functionality? Unfortunately, I haven’t been able to find any documentation on this matter.

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you,

Marco

entry.title: WFA on HANA enablement
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 ,

I have a few questions on the enablement process for WFA on HANA.

In a recent project I have started I have bumped into quite a few differences when compared to past projects.

1. "Customer has Workforce Analytics license" checkbox in Provisioning

Is this checkbox something we as consultants need to switch? I have never had to do this before.

I always checked and the switch was on and that simply told me that ok there is a license in place, I can go in and import the template etc.

In my most recent project I had to actually go there and click this (was not sure that the license is actually in place as customer just recently bought it and agreed it will be in place with X date).

Then for another customer, I was asked what are the prerequisites and enablement steps and they assured me they have a license, then in Provisioning I have noticed again it is not switched.

2. "Use WFA URL Set" in Provisioning

Again, something I have never touched before myself.

In test after the above mentioned checkbox has been marked we had some other issues that SAP engineering helped with, but no mention of this "Use WFA URL Set" option.

Since we had some issues in test, I advised we should check production as soon as possible to simply enable and make sure we do not bump into the same.

In there we could not find WFA on HANA Data Factory after enabling the checkbox from #1 above so we have found this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2708026/E

Again, this is not something I have ever touched before in any other WFA on HANA projects, is this some setting I should always check?

3. Measure Views

Always after importing the WFA on HANA template, I open a support ticket with SAP to request that they upload the Measure Views. Always quick and easy.

This time around, I have received the following reply:

"Good day! Enable Measure Views for WFA is part of implementation of WFA. It is done by either your implementation partner or our consultant.
You can ask this on an ask this on jam page/ workzone for consulting. 
They are responsible for this, and they will be able to help you with that."

Even more strange, the Measure Views are in place.

Again, is this correct? Should I come here and request this instead of SAP Support?

 

What I am trying to understand is, for 1, 2 and 3 above, did anything change in the enablement process for WFA on HANA and I have just blindly missed the information on it?

 

4. "IsAnalyticsInEc">True<

I have mentioned above that we had some issue after importing the template (advanced version)

After one month of back and forth with SAP engineering they have concluded that our issue came from the template having the "IsAnalyticsInEc">True< setting and turning it off resolved the issue.

I do not know what this setting does, they have mentioned it is related to some legacy feature that went away in 2020 or so.

Now, as I am using the template provided in the original Jam Group, does the template actually have this setting on? And if so, why would it not impact all customers?

I have only once before heard of this setting for another customer, where I had a very different issue caused by it that SAP engineering has fixed in the same way, turning this setting off.

 

Many thanks in advance for your answers.

Kind regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Integration of Google reCAPTCHA
entry.content:
Hey there, 

This should absolutely be doable for the web build for sure. Below is a custom js that would essentially achieve the desired goal: 

```

const CLIENT_KEY = 'your-site-key';
/**
* @fileoverview This script dynamically loads Google's reCAPTCHA v3, executes it,
* and retrieves a verification token for backend validation.
*/
const { document } = globalThis;
const RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT_ID = "RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT";
const RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT_URL = `https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api.js?render=${CLIENT_KEY}`;

/**
* Loads the Google reCAPTCHA script dynamically if it is not already loaded.
* Ensures the script is only added once to the page.
*
* @returns {Promise<void>} Resolves when the script is loaded.
*/
async function loadRecaptcha() {
// If the script is already present, just resolve
if (document.getElementById(RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT_ID)) {
console.log("reCAPTCHA script already loaded.");
return;
}

return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
const script = document.createElement("script");
script.id = RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT_ID;
script.src=RECAPTCHA_SCRIPT_URL;
script.async = true;
script.defer = true;
script.onload = () => resolve();
script.onerror = () => reject(new Error("Failed to load reCAPTCHA script"));

document.head.appendChild(script);
});
}

/**
* Executes Google's reCAPTCHA for a specific action and retrieves a token.
*
* @Param {string} action - The name of the action (e.g., "submit", "login").
* @returns {Promise<string>} Resolves with the generated token.
*/
async function executeRecaptcha(action) {
await loadRecaptcha();

return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
if (typeof grecaptcha === "undefined") {
return reject(new Error("grecaptcha is not available"));
}

grecaptcha.ready(() => {
grecaptcha.execute(CLIENT_KEY, { action: action })
.then(token => resolve(token))
.catch(error => reject(error));
});
});
}

// Example usage: Get a reCAPTCHA token for 'submit' action
try {
const token = await executeRecaptcha("submit");
// Now you can pass `token` to the rest of your nodered logic
return [0, { token }];
} catch (error) {
return [1, { error }];
};


```

The return values are prepared for a 'custom js' function that has these outputs specified: 

And so the whole flow would look like this: 

PS. You can also pass in the site_key or client_key as an input parameter to the custom js flow of Build Apps.

Hope this helps, if something is not clear enough, or was I too vague, ping me here. 

Best regards, 
Mihály Tóth 

PS2. One could probably wonder why there is a 'SCRIPT_ID' and why is that dynamic appending. This is to ensure that one script is only added once to the app, which could cause issues in both the built final app, and also the web preview.

entry.title: Re: Overwrite dimension filter using SAC Add-in
entry.content:
The table 2 formula should be 

SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER("Table2","Period", D3:D5, 4,1)

The list of members to filter on should be a range formula, not individual cells. As long as the date formats are the same between the models your idea should work. 

Other option would be to use SAP.GETDATA() with your 2 different data sources. Accounts A, B would reference your Live Model source and Account C would reference your import model source.

entry.title: Re: Screen Personas Update Server restart
entry.content:
I have never heard about customers stating such things.

A Chrome update will not dictate a Personas upgrade. On the other hand, it is important to keep Personas itself updated regularly by implementing all released notes for it, especially if experiencing any problem. However, this has nothing to do with Chrome updates.

Similarly, a Personas upgrade itself will not cause "a server reboot". Which server is meant here?
It is recommended to perform SP upgrades when users who rely on flavors are not active, but this doesn't mean a requirement of bouncing the SAP system.

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA enablement
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 , here is response to your questions:

1. "Customer has Workforce Analytics license" checkbox in Provisioning - this is required to turn on so you can see the WFA on HANA Data Factory menu.  Usually this is already checked when someone provisioned the BizX instance and the customer has license for it.  It may also be turned on if customer want to have WFA demo cube installed in the instance.  But there is a change of process now that WFA demo cube is not always installed during BizX instance provisioned process so probably that's why you see some instances do not have this turn-on.

I am unsure if there is a background job that run to turn this off if customer has no license, you have to check with support.

2. "Use WFA URL Set" in Provisioning - this is a required setting for Advanced Reporting.  Again, usually setup when BizX instance get provisioned because EC also used Advanced Reporting for its reports.  However, since we now have embedded story, customer can opt to use story instead of Canvas report so it is possible this is not setup for certain customers.  As this is part of Advanced Reporting setup, it is not mentioned in WFA on HANA documentation.  But having Advanced Reporting is a prerequisite to implement WFA on HANA and this is mentioned in the WFA on HANA Welcome - Read me first documentation.  Support should be able to help with the setup.

3. Measure Views - Support should be able to upload the measure template.  If there was WFA demo cube installed in the instance before, there's a high chance that measure template has been uploaded before.  It is mentioned in the WFA on HANA Welcome - Read me first documentation to contact support to get measure template uploaded.  Please do not request for measure template upload in community as the people who monitor this forum may not have access to the target DC and instance to upload the measure template.  

If you get different response from support, most likely those who new in the support team or never handle WFA on HANA tickets before do not know the exact process.

4. "IsAnalyticsInEc">True< - I've checked both starter and advanced template and it is setup as False for this setting.  I am not sure why Engineering said it is caused by the template.  The menu to turn off this setting is not part of WFA on HANA Data Factory and there is a setting we need to turn off in the provisioning:

Need to run "Delete Cube" to remove the cube. 

Most of the time this setting already turned on for the instance.  It could be from some setup from Upgrade Center.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @x-eandrei ,

Yes, for Non-EC customers, there is no configuration to automatically update displayName. It is auto generated when create new employee, after that system will not auto populate it. Customer need to either import it, manage it on UI or integrate with master data system to maintain employee's displayName. 

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: Exploring GraphQL and REST for SAP Developers
entry.content:
Great blog

My understanding is GraphQL is really useful when data needs to be retrieved from multiple backends and consumed in single application.

Rather than creating multiple point to point connections, GraphQL take the complexity of multiple backends and prvides single facde layer

Regards
